title: Patterns Overview — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlassian patterns solve common user problems by composing components and foundations. 2025 update: AI/Rovo interaction patterns added as first-class patterns. Existing patterns cover: messages (inline, flags, banners), empty states, loading states, and AI chat interfaces. Patterns are separate from components — they prescribe which components combine and why.

title: Messages Pattern — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlassian Messages pattern distinguishes: Inline message (contextual, inside content), Flag (toast notification — top-right, auto-dismiss for success, persistent for error), and Banner (full-width, page-level, above navigation for critical system messages). Specifies: success flags auto-dismiss in 5s, error flags require manual dismiss, banners are for outages/degradation only.

title: Autocomplete Design: 9 UX Best Practices — Baymard Institute
insights: Baymard research (18,000+ user tests): only 19% of sites implement all best practices. Critical findings: (1) Show query match highlighted in suggestion, (2) Show category/type label ('in Electronics'), (3) Differentiate search term suggestions from product name suggestions, (4) Show image thumbnails for product autocomplete, (5) Max 8 suggestions, (6) Don't autocomplete typos into final query. Qualitative finding: users trust highlighted matches more.

title: Dialog Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon dialog pattern taxonomy: Transactional (requires user action to proceed), Passive (informational), Acknowledgment (user must confirm they read). Composition: Modal + ModalHeader + ModalBody + ModalFooter. Destructive modals use danger variant button, cancel takes focus (not delete button). ARIA: aria-modal=true, focus trap.

title: Empty States Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon prescribes three empty state types: first-use (new user, no data yet), no-results (search/filter returned nothing), and error (data failed to load). Each has: illustration (optional), heading (mandatory), body text (max 3 lines), and CTA (optional). Anti-pattern: showing illustration in small tiles/toasts. Content guidance: solution-oriented message, not failure-focused.

title: Filtering Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon filtering taxonomy: Single filter, Multi-filter batch (multiple at once, no auto-apply), Inline filter (within table column headers). Applied filters shown as tags that can be dismissed. Filter panel can be sidebar or inline. Batch filtering: user sets all filters then clicks Apply — reduces unnecessary API calls.

title: Forms Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon forms pattern covers: simple (single column, single page), complex (multi-column, tabbed sections), wizard (multi-step with progress indicator). Layout rules: 2-column max on desktop, label above field, help text below field, error message replaces help text on validation fail. All required fields marked, not optional.

title: Notification Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon notification taxonomy: toast (temporary, top-right, 5-7s auto-dismiss), inline (persistent, inside content), banner (full-width, page-level), action notification (includes CTA link). Two styles: high-contrast (urgent) and low-contrast (informational). Toast duration formula: 50ms x char count, min 2s, max 7s.

title: Patterns Overview — Carbon Design System (IBM)
insights: Carbon v11 pattern catalog: Dialogs, Empty states, Forms, Global header, Login, Notifications, Search, Filtering, Loading. Each pattern documents: overview, usage guidance, component composition, content guidance, accessibility, and live code. IBM definition: patterns are best-practice solutions for how a user achieves a goal.

title: Validation Pattern — GOV.UK Design System
insights: GOV.UK validation: validate on submit, not on keystroke. Error summary at top of page (links to each field error). Error message inline next to each field. Messages worded same in summary and inline. Pattern: wait for submit before showing errors — avoids anxiety during typing. Error message format: [Field name] must be [requirement].

title: Carbon Design System — IBM (GitHub)
insights: Carbon GitHub repo: 7.9k stars. v11 active, v10 in maintenance. React components + Vanilla + Angular + Vue wrappers. Pattern documentation in Storybook and carbondesignsystem.com. Pattern contribution guide: new patterns require a problem statement, usage examples, accessibility audit, and content guidance before merging. Community patterns maintained separately at carbondesignsystem.com/community/patterns/.

title: Patterns — Shopify Polaris
insights: Polaris documents pattern compositions: Resource Index Layout, Resource Details Layout, App Settings Layout, Date Picking (list and range variants), Error Messages, Loading States, and Saving. Each pattern specifies the exact component set to compose (e.g., IndexFilter + IndexTable + Page + Card + Badge + ChoiceList for Resource Index), plus content guidance and interaction spec.

title: App Settings Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Settings page pattern: BlockStack + Card + InlineGrid + Box. Sections separated into logical groups, each Card containing a SettingToggle or form fields. Contextual save bar appears only when there are unsaved changes. No side-nav tabs — single scrolling page for app settings with fewer than ~10 settings sections.

title: Resource Details Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Companion pattern to resource index. Specifies layout: Page (title + breadcrumb + primary action) + two-column InlineGrid (primary content 2/3 + metadata sidebar 1/3). Uses Card for content sections, ContextualSaveBar for unsaved-changes state, and Banner for page-level alerts.

title: Resource Index Layout — Shopify Polaris
insights: Canonical pattern for list pages: uses IndexTable with bulk actions, IndexFilter for faceted filtering, Page component for header actions, Pagination component, and Badge for status. Specifies sticky header, skeleton state on initial load, empty state when no items, and search-match highlighting. Anti-pattern: using Card-wrapped forms for list pages.

title: Refactoring UI — Adam Wathan & Steve Schoger
insights: Refactoring UI core principles: design in grayscale first (forces hierarchy via spacing/contrast/typography before color), build 5-9 shade palettes per hue, constrained spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64/96/128px), type scale (12/14/16/18/20/24/30/36/48/60/72px). Pattern library includes 20+ component categories, 200+ component styles. 'Start with too much whitespace' — remove, never add.

title: Smart Interface Design Patterns — Vitaly Friedman (Smashing Magazine)
insights: Vitaly Friedman's 40-lesson course covers 100 patterns with live examples and 15h video. Pattern categories: navigation, forms, tables, data display, feedback, e-commerce, search, accessibility. Course includes 165-page UX checklist deck updated annually. Key patterns: complex data tables with nested filters, combobox vs multi-select vs listbox decision tree, autocomplete UX (5-step framework), mega-menus, infinite scroll vs pagination.

title: Design Pattern Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG research: a well-documented pattern contains: problem statement, solution, when to use (context), when NOT to use (anti-patterns), visual example, interaction spec, content guidance, accessibility notes, and code. Patterns without when not to use documentation result in misuse.

title: Design Systems 101 — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG defines component library vs pattern library: component library = atomic UI elements; pattern library = compositions of elements into reusable page sections or flows. Research finding: teams with a pattern library ship 34% faster and have 28% fewer UI inconsistencies.

title: Designing Empty States — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG 3 guidelines for empty states: (1) Explain why the space is empty and what it means. (2) Provide a constructive next step — primary CTA directly addresses the emptiness. (3) Match the visual design to the emotional context. Research: 68% of users abandon apps that show empty states without guidance.

title: Wizards: Definition and Design Recommendations — NNG
insights: NNG: wizards are best for long, unfamiliar tasks users complete rarely (setup, onboarding, compliance). NOT suited for tasks users do daily. Research: wizards reduce errors by 22% for complex tasks. Guidelines: 3-10 steps, allow back navigation without data loss, show step count, validate per step.

title: Wizard Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly wizard: vertical step nav on left (each step labelled, status: not-started, in-progress, complete, error), content area on right. Back/Next buttons in footer. Cancel link. Progressive validation: validate current step before allowing Next. Max 8 steps recommended.

title: Patterns — PatternFly (Red Hat)
insights: PatternFly patterns catalog: Bulk selection, Dashboard, Filters, Sticky header, Table with actions, Wizard, Empty state, Error state, Login page. Each pattern: usage guidance + component composition + accessibility. PatternFly is Red Hat's enterprise open-source system.

title: Bulk Selection Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly bulk selection uses a SplitButton in the toolbar: left side selects/deselects all on page, dropdown offers Select all (N items), Select page, Select none. Selected count displayed. Bulk action buttons appear in toolbar (not inline rows). Three selection scopes: page-level, global, row-level.

title: Filters Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly filter variants: text entry, single select, checkbox select, date range picker. Filter toolbar sits above data list/table. Applied filters shown as chip groups below toolbar — each chip shows attribute+value, has X to remove. Clear all filters button resets all. FilterGroup (multiple filter types in one toolbar row).

title: Data Table Design UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper enterprise data table analysis: sorting (column chevrons, secondary sort via Shift+click), bulk selection (header checkbox = page, toolbar action = apply), inline editing (cell-level vs row-level), row expansion (expandable rows for related data), column resizing, column reordering, sticky first/last columns, empty state, and loading skeleton. Anti-pattern: putting bulk actions inside row dropdown menus.

title: Filter UX Design Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper filter pattern analysis: side panel (best for 5+ filter categories), top horizontal bar (3-5 quick filters), inline column filters (table-specific). Applied filter chips below filter bar. Search within filter panel for long lists. Batch vs instant apply: batch for performance-heavy results, instant for <200ms response. Hierarchical filters (parent/child category). Mobile: filter drawer (full-screen slide-up).

title: Combobox Pattern — WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
insights: W3C WAI-ARIA combobox pattern: role=combobox on input, aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete (none/list/inline/both), aria-controls (listbox ID). Keyboard: Down arrow opens/moves to first option, Enter selects, Escape closes. Autocomplete variants: list (shows options), inline (completes in input field), both. Required for all custom select/autocomplete components. Pattern includes complete code example.

title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern — WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
insights: W3C WAI-ARIA APG modal pattern: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby (header), aria-describedby (body). Focus management: focus moves to first focusable element on open, Tab cycles within dialog, Escape closes. Background content: aria-hidden=true. Focus returns to trigger element on close. Two variants: dialog (modal) and alertdialog (for confirmations requiring immediate response). Code examples with focus trap implementation.

title: Detail Page Pattern — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design detail page pattern: PageHeader (breadcrumb + title + subtitle + tags + actions) + content area. Content layout: basic info section (descriptive list or form), table section (related records), tab section (multiple categories of related info). Action placement: primary actions in PageHeader, secondary actions in tabs. Edit mode: inline form replaces description list. Mobile: PageHeader stacks vertically.

title: Layout — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design 6 layout templates: (1) Top navigation + content area, (2) Left sidebar + content, (3) Left sidebar + top nav + content, (4) Top nav + left sidebar + content, (5) Inline menu (no sidebar, flat), (6) Top nav + mixed (complex). Grid: 24-column, gutter 8/16/24px. Breakpoints: xs<576, sm≥576, md≥768, lg≥992, xl≥1200, xxl≥1600.

title: Design Patterns Overview — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design pattern documentation: 6 page templates (home, dashboard, list, table, details, form). Pattern categories: navigation design (standard layouts), data entry (form types), data display (table, card, list), feedback (modal, notification, message), and overview pages. Ant Design Pro (out-of-box): 20+ full page templates including dashboard, list, form, detail, exception pages. Enterprise-focused: built for internal tools and admin panels.

title: Loading Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon loading taxonomy: spinner (short indeterminate waits <10s), skeleton (page/section initial load), inline loading (button/action-triggered), progress bar (determinate, file upload/batch). Skeleton appears on initial render before data arrives; skeleton nodes mirror exact final layout dimensions.

title: Search Pattern — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon search pattern: global search (site-wide) vs local search (within page/component). Search + Typeahead + recent-searches layer. Results page uses DataTable or structured list. Search is not filtering — filtering refines an existing set, search queries a backend. Scope indicator (Search all products) reduces ambiguity.

title: Inline Edit Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape inline edit: pencil icon on cell hover, click to enter edit mode (cell becomes FormField with input). Save via Enter or checkmark, cancel via Escape or X. Validation shown inline below cell input. Only one cell edits at a time.

title: Split View Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape split view: table/card list (left 60%) + detail panel (right 40%). Side panel appears when row is selected. Avoids full-page navigation for quick detail inspection. Panel has SplitPanel component with close X. Anti-pattern: using split view when detail content requires deep editing.

title: Table View Pattern — AWS Cloudscape
insights: Cloudscape table view: full-page variant of Table component. Sticky header recommended. Toolbar: search + filter + column selector + refresh. Bulk selection: page-scope by default, Select all N items global scope. Split panel (60/40) optional for row-detail preview without navigation.

title: Addresses Pattern — GOV.UK Design System
insights: GOV.UK address pattern: address lookup (postcode lookup API + manual fallback) preferred over multiple free-text fields for UK addresses. Multiple text inputs only when country is known. Fields: address line 1, address line 2 (optional), town/city, postcode. Never combine city and postcode in one field.

title: Patterns — US Web Design System (USWDS)
insights: USWDS patterns go beyond components — they are documented solutions for complex tasks. Documented patterns: Complete a complex form, Create a user profile, Enter an address, Request personal info, Connect communication preferences, Choose a language. Each has research evidence, content guidance, accessibility requirements. All WCAG 2.1 AA compliant.

title: Complete a Complex Form — USWDS Patterns
insights: USWDS complex form patterns: Progress easily (step indicator), Understand and meet requirements (content strategy before first field), Review content (review step before submit), Work across sessions (save and return). Content guidance: one thing per page (only ask what's needed for this step). Error strategy: submit-time validation, never keystroke. 508 compliance mandatory — all form patterns tested with AT.

title: Progress Easily — USWDS Multi-Step Form Pattern
insights: USWDS multi-step form pattern: Step indicator shows current step, completed steps (checkmark), total steps. Each step = one page. Navigation: Back button (preserve entered data), Continue button. Save and return later: store session data and issue a return URL. Research finding: users complete complex forms 40% faster when progress is visible.

title: Fluent UI — Microsoft Developer
insights: Fluent UI official: React component library for Microsoft 365. Documented compositions: DataGrid with selection, filtering, and custom cell rendering; Dialog with form content; CommandBar for action bars; SearchBox with suggestions; Panel (side drawer) for detail views. 60+ components. TypeScript-first with full accessibility built in. Pattern documentation includes Teams-specific usage guidelines (Teams app, M365 add-ins). Fluent 2 unifies web, desktop, and mobile component APIs.

title: Helios Design System — HashiCorp (GitHub)
insights: Helios GitHub repo (hashicorp/design-system). Ember.js + React components. Patterns documented: table multi-select (3 selection scopes), form patterns (8px grid spacing, label-above convention), navigation (sidebar + top bar). Components: HDS prefix. Figma: HDS Product Foundations v2.0 + HDS Product Patterns v2.0 (detachable templates). Used across HashiCorp Cloud Platform products.

title: Form Patterns — HashiCorp Helios
insights: Helios form patterns: spacing follows 8px grid between sections. Form layout rules: label above input, helper text below input, error message replaces helper text. Groups of related fields use FormField wrapper. Multi-column forms (2-col max on desktop). Required fields marked with asterisk.

title: Table Multi-Select Pattern — HashiCorp Helios
insights: Helios defines three selection scopes: global (all results via SplitButton in toolbar), page-level (header checkbox), row-level (individual row checkbox). Selection persistence across pagination is complex — default is page-scope only. Bulk actions appear in toolbar when selection > 0. Selected count badge displayed.

title: Kanban (Visual Board) Pattern — ServiceNow Horizon
insights: Horizon Visual Board (Kanban): columns = workflow stages. Card = entity (incident, request, candidate). Card surface: title, assignee avatar, priority icon, due date, tag chips (max 3). Drag and drop: cards reorder within column and across columns. Column WIP limit: visual indicator when limit exceeded (column header turns orange). Quick actions on card: reassign, escalate, close. Swimlanes optional for grouping by team.

title: Navigation Pattern — ServiceNow Horizon
insights: Horizon (ServiceNow) navigation pattern: Workspace app = left nav bar (collapsed 56px icon-only, expanded 240px icon+label) + top navigation bar (app switcher, user menu, global search). Patterns = 'recipes' distinct from components. Navigation levels: L1 (sidebar icons), L2 (expanded section), L3 (contextual side panel). AI integration: AI-powered quick actions in navigation panel.

title: Material Design 3 — Google
insights: Material Design 3 (Material You) patterns: navigation patterns (NavigationBar bottom 3-5 items, NavigationDrawer side 5+ items, NavigationRail for desktop), search (SearchBar + SearchView composition), bottom sheets (modal and persistent variants), dialogs (basic, full-screen). M3 Expressive (2025): 35 new shapes, shape morphing, motion physics system.

title: Seven New UX Patterns for Carbon — IBM Carbon Design
insights: IBM Carbon 7 new patterns: (1) Dialogs (transactional/passive/acknowledgment), (2) Empty states (3 types), (3) Forms (simple/complex/wizard), (4) Global header (navigation + search + notifications), (5) Login (credentials + multi-factor), (6) Notifications (4 types + placement rules), (7) Search (global + local + filtering). Each published with full component spec, accessibility notes, and live code. Part of Carbon v11 pattern expansion.

title: Date Picking (Date List) — Shopify Polaris
insights: Single date selection from a list of presets via Popover + OptionList + Button. No calendar shown — suited for relative date selection (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 days). Lightweight pattern for dashboard filter scenarios.

title: Date Picking (Date Range) — Shopify Polaris
insights: Date range selection recipe: Popover + Button trigger + InlineGrid (two-column: OptionList of presets + DatePicker calendar) + BlockStack + TextField for custom range entry. Presets like Last 7 days, Last 30 days, Custom range. Popover closes on selection.

title: Primer — GitHub Design System
insights: Primer documents UI patterns for: filtering/search (in repositories, PRs, issues), empty states (Blankslate — icon + heading + description + CTA), dialogs (Dialog with focus trap), navigation (UnderlineNav, TreeView for file explorer, ActionMenu for overflow), and PageLayout (two/three column with sidebar).

title: Autocomplete UX: Five Simple Steps — Smart Interface Design Patterns
insights: 5-step autocomplete framework: (1) Show max 8 suggestions (not 10+), (2) Highlight query in suggestion, (3) Show category/type label with suggestion, (4) Include recent searches at top, (5) Keyboard nav (arrow keys + Enter). Enterprise autocomplete: show entity type icon, include people/docs/tasks cross-entity search. Do not show typos in recent search history — show corrected query.

title: Combobox vs. Multiselect vs. Listbox — Smart Interface Design Patterns
insights: Decision tree: listbox (≤7 options, always visible), dropdown select (7-15 options, space constrained), combobox (15+ options, type-to-filter), multiselect combobox (multiple values from large set). Multiselect pattern: selected values shown as tags/chips above or inside input. Combobox: WAI-ARIA combobox role, aria-autocomplete, aria-expanded. Never use native <select multiple> in enterprise apps.

title: Spectrum — Adobe Design System
insights: Adobe Spectrum 2 (2025 release) organises system into: foundation, components, patterns, content, and voice/tone. Patterns are component sets solving common design problems. Spectrum 2 brings adaptive color (light/dark/spectrum themes), density modes, and AI interaction patterns for Creative Cloud.

title: Patterns — Salesforce Lightning Design System 2
insights: SLDS 2 pattern library covers: inline editing (table cells, form fields), read/edit state transitions, action menus (contextual row actions), related lists, activity timeline, dueling picklist, lookup (combobox with search), and rich text editor. Enterprise HR domain: candidate record pattern uses activity timeline + related list + inline edit composition.

title: Design Guidelines — Salesforce Lightning Design System 2
insights: SLDS 2 renames guidelines from SLDS 1 to patterns. SLDS 2 separates structure from visual design via styling hooks (CSS custom properties). Patterns cover: data entry, data display, navigation, messaging, and loading. SLDS 2 Spring 25: new CSS framework, deeper customisation.

title: Accordions on Desktop — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG accordion research: on desktop, accordions slow users because content is hidden. Use when: users need overview of sections and will read selectively; page has dense information; content is reference (FAQ, settings) not task flow. Allow multiple sections open simultaneously on desktop.

title: Breadcrumbs: 11 Design Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG breadcrumb research: location breadcrumbs are most common and useful; path breadcrumbs are confusing. Place below global nav, above page title. Current page should not be a link. Separator: > or /. On mobile: show only parent page. Never use breadcrumbs as primary navigation.

title: Data Tables: Four Major User Tasks — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG 4 user tasks for tables: look up specific value, compare values across rows, understand trends, filter to a subset. Design implications: sortable columns required for compare task; sticky first column for wide tables; row hover highlight for look-up task; clear pagination. Headers must be descriptive not abbreviated.

title: Listboxes vs. Dropdown Lists — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG: use listbox (always-visible list) when ≤7 options. Use dropdown when >7 options or screen is constrained. Combobox (type-to-filter) when >15 options. Multi-select: use checkbox list or multi-select listbox. Never use dropdown for binary yes/no or fewer than 3 options.

title: Skeleton Screens 101 — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG skeleton screen research: skeleton screens improve perceived performance by 20-30% vs spinners. Should mirror exact final layout. Animate with shimmer (left-to-right gradient) not pulse. Use for initial page load — not for subsequent data updates.

title: Bulk Actions: 3 Design Guidelines — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNG 3 bulk action guidelines: (1) Show contextual action bar when items are selected. (2) Show the count of selected items prominently. (3) For destructive bulk actions, always require confirmation. Floating contextual toolbar outperforms static toolbar in usability tests.

title: Empty State — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly empty state: EmptyStateIcon (large, themed), Title, EmptyStateBody, EmptyStateActions (primary button + secondary link). Size variants: xs (no icon), sm, lg, xl. Use xl for full-page empty state, sm/xs within cards or widgets. Content: No {noun} found as heading + explain why + what to do.

title: Inline Edit Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly inline edit: table row edit (pencil icon toggle, row enters edit mode, kebab replaced by check+close icons), form field edit (pencil icon next to field value). Always show check (save) and X (cancel). Validation: inline error below field in edit mode.

title: Modal Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly modal sizes: sm (446px), md (576px), lg (836px), xl (1176px). ModalHeader has close X always. Destructive modal: danger Button for primary, Cancel for secondary — but Cancel takes focus on open (not danger). No nesting modals.

title: Dashboard Pattern — PatternFly
insights: PatternFly dashboard card types: aggregate status card (count + icon + color-coded severity), trend card (sparkline + delta %), utilization chart (donut or bar), events/activity card (timeline list). Grid layout: 4-column at full width, 2-column at medium. Cards never exceed 1/4 width unless a feature card (1/2).

title: Dashboard Design UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper dashboard patterns: overview cards (KPI strip at top, 4-6 max), trend charts (sparkline or mini line chart in card), alert/notification section (at-a-glance status), data table (secondary detail). Widget grid: draggable/resizable for customizable dashboards. F-pattern layout: most critical metric top-left. Anti-pattern: 'data vomit' — too many metrics without hierarchy.

title: Data Table Bulk Actions — Pencil & Paper (sub-section)
insights: P&P bulk actions in enterprise tables: floating bottom action bar emerges when selection > 0. Bar contains: '{N} selected', primary bulk actions (2-3 max visible), overflow menu for more, deselect all. After bulk action: success toast with undo. Row checkbox column: sticky-left, 40px width. Shift+click for range selection. Ctrl+Click for non-contiguous. 'Select all X items in table' when page-select used on paginated table.

title: Mobile Filter UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Mobile filter patterns: full-screen filter drawer (swipe-up/button trigger), chips row (horizontal scroll, visible applied filters), sort+filter combined bottom sheet. Mobile-specific: show applied filter count on trigger button, 'Show results (N)' confirmation button. No instant-apply on mobile — batch apply on close. Sticky filter bar on scroll for always-accessible refinement.

title: Error Messages — Shopify Polaris (legacy)
insights: Error pattern taxonomy: inline field error (InlineError component under field), form-level error summary (Banner type=critical at page top), page-level error (full-page error state). Specifies that inline errors use red text + icon, error summary links to individual fields, and error messages must state the problem and the fix.

title: Settings — Material Design (M1)
insights: Material Design settings patterns (canonical baseline): all settings under single 'Settings' label. Categories visible on first screen without drilling. Setting rows: title + current value/state + control. Boolean = switch toggle. Single-select = current value shown in subtitle (opens RadioGroup dialog on tap). Multi-select = opens CheckboxList dialog. Subsettings screen: back arrow navigation. Destructive settings at bottom, red text.

title: Design Patterns Catalog — UI-Patterns.com
insights: UI-Patterns.com catalogs 80+ interface design patterns with problem/solution format. Key patterns: Autocomplete, Breadcrumbs, Calendar Picker, Cards, Continuous Scrolling, DataGrid, Dropdown, Forgiving Format, InplaceEditor, LazyRegistration, Pagination, PayWall, ProgressiveDisclosure, Settings, TableFilter, Wizard.

title: Inplace Editor Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: Inplace Editor: problem = navigating to edit page interrupts reading flow; solution = click to edit in situ. Three variants: text click-to-edit, icon-triggered (pencil on hover), inline form. Save options: auto-save on blur, explicit save button, Enter/Escape key. Anti-pattern: no visual affordance that text is editable.

title: Paywall Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: Paywall: soft paywall (content partially visible, blurred/faded + upgrade CTA), hard paywall (feature completely inaccessible, locked icon + CTA). Best practice: frame as unlocking not blocking. Show feature value before paywall. Include social proof and risk reversal.

title: Settings Pattern — UI-Patterns.com
insights: Settings: should be named Settings (not Options/Preferences). Layout: side-nav tabs for complex apps (10+ sections), single scrolling page for simple apps. Group by: account, notifications, privacy, integrations, billing. Destructive settings at bottom, visually separated.

title: Checkout UX Best Practices 2025 — Baymard Institute
insights: Baymard 2025 checkout research: average checkout has 11.3 fields (optimal is ~8). Multi-step checkout (address → shipping → payment → review) outperforms accordion checkout for new users. Progress indicators reduce abandonment. Field formatting: card number should auto-space every 4 digits, expiry as MM/YY not MM and YYYY. 35% conversion improvement possible from checkout UX fixes alone.

title: How to Build a Remarkable Command Palette — Superhuman
insights: Superhuman (the email app famous for its UX) on command palettes: fuzzy matching should rank by (1) exact prefix match, (2) word-start match, (3) arbitrary position match. Show keyboard shortcut for each command so users discover shortcuts. Group commands: navigation, actions, settings. Recent commands prioritized. Empty state: show most-used commands. Cmd+K as the universal trigger in modern apps.

title: Multi File Upload — UK Ministry of Justice Design System
insights: MOJ multi-file upload pattern: drag-and-drop zone + browse button, individual file rows with name + size + progress bar + remove button. States per file: waiting, uploading (progress bar), complete (green checkmark), error (red X + error message). Validation: file type (accept attribute), file size (client-side check before upload), total size limit, file count limit. Accessible: works without JS (standard input fallback).

title: Awesome Design Systems — alexpate (GitHub)
insights: Curated list of 200+ design systems with 21.8k GitHub stars. Categorized by: open source vs proprietary, web vs mobile, industry. Notable pattern-rich systems cataloged: Polaris (Shopify), Carbon (IBM), Atlassian, Primer (GitHub), Lightning (Salesforce), Spectrum (Adobe), Fluent (Microsoft), PatternFly (Red Hat), Helios (HashiCorp), Cloudscape (AWS), USWDS (US Gov), GOV.UK, Horizon (ServiceNow).

title: Ant Design Pro — GitHub
insights: Ant Design Pro: 20+ page templates, 10+ business components. Templates: dashboard (analytics + monitor + workplace), form (basic, step-by-step, advanced), list (standard, card, search), profile (basic, advanced), result (success, fail), exception (403, 404, 500), account (center, settings). 17k+ GitHub stars. Business components: comments, footer toolbar, exception, tag select, standard table.

title: Essential Resources for Complex Data Tables — Stéphanie Walter
insights: Curated resource collection for enterprise data tables. Key referenced patterns: fixed vs flexible column widths, right-align numbers/currency, left-align text, center-align icons/status. Responsive table strategies: horizontal scroll (simplest), priority columns (hide low-priority on mobile), card transformation (rows become cards). Sticky header + first column for large grids. Row height: compact (32px), default (48px), relaxed (64px).

title: Scorecard Overview — Greenhouse ATS
insights: Greenhouse ATS scorecard UI: structured evaluation form per interview stage. Components: Attributes section (competencies to evaluate), Rating scale (Strong No / No / Mixed / Yes / Strong Yes), Overall recommendation, Additional notes (per attribute + summary). Candidate overview shows interviewer scorecards in aggregate (color-coded dots). Calibration: all scorecards visible post-interview in read-only mode. Forms configured per job stage, not per job.

title: Sidebar Component — shadcn/ui
insights: shadcn Sidebar: built as a collapsible composition using Collapsible + Sheet (mobile). Sidebar states: expanded (240px, icon+label), collapsed (64px, icon only), mobile (Sheet overlay). Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+B to toggle. SidebarProvider wraps app for context. SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton sub-components. Mobile: sidebar becomes Sheet drawer. Preserves collapse state via cookie.

title: Thoughtful Dashboards for B2B SaaS — UX Collective
insights: B2B SaaS dashboard design process: (1) Define user tasks (not just metrics), (2) Identify primary vs secondary metrics, (3) Establish visual hierarchy (primary KPIs largest), (4) Choose correct chart type (time series → line, composition → donut, comparison → bar, distribution → histogram), (5) Test scan path (eye-tracking). Anti-patterns: more than 7 KPIs without grouping, pie charts with >5 segments, stacked bars for time series comparison.

title: Designing Better Destructive Action Modals — UX Psychology
insights: Destructive modal patterns: standard 2-button (Cancel + Delete), confirmation input (type resource name to confirm, e.g., GitHub repo delete), checkbox acknowledgment ('I understand this cannot be undone'), delayed enable (confirm button enabled after 3s countdown). Research: confirmation input reduces accidental deletes by 87%. Use confirmation input for irreversible high-stakes actions only (not for recoverable deletions).

title: When to Use Modal vs Non-Modal Dialog — A11y Collective
insights: Modal vs non-modal decision: modal (blocks background, requires resolution) for confirmations, destructive actions, critical input; non-modal (allows background interaction) for supplementary information, contextual help, floating toolbars. Dialog roles: role=dialog (modal), role=alertdialog (critical, auto-focused, e.g., session timeout). Alert vs alertdialog: alert = no interaction required (toast), alertdialog = requires user response. ARIA live regions for dynamic content.

title: Data Table — shadcn/ui
insights: shadcn/ui data table: headless composition via TanStack Table + shadcn Table component. Standard composition: column definitions (with cell renderers, header renderers, sort config), toolbar (Input search + DropdownMenu column visibility), data rows, footer pagination. Faceted filter popover pattern: DropdownMenuCheckboxItem per filter option, Badge showing selection count. Enterprise variant: server-side sort/filter/paginate, React.useReducer for state.

title: Designing Effective Breadcrumbs — Smashing Magazine
insights: Smashing Magazine breadcrumb deep-dive: 3 types (location, path, attribute). Location breadcrumbs are universally applicable. Attribute breadcrumbs (show product filters) degrade to location breadcrumbs on mobile. Separator choice: '>' most discoverable for users. Schema.org breadcrumb markup for SEO. Never truncate breadcrumbs on mobile — use back button pattern instead. ARIA: nav role + aria-label='Breadcrumb' + aria-current='page' on last item.

title: Design Patterns — Smashing Magazine
insights: Smashing Magazine design patterns category: high-quality research-backed articles. Key patterns covered (2024-2025): mega-menus, complex data tables, mobile filter patterns, notification UX timing, date/time pickers, command palette evolution, AI chat interface patterns, onboarding flows. Monthly pattern deep-dives. Smart Interface Design Patterns course (Vitaly Friedman) paired with article content.

title: Empty States: The Most Overlooked Aspect of UX — Toptal
insights: Toptal empty state deep-dive: 3 emotional contexts = first use (educational + welcoming), no results (helpful + diagnostic), error (reassuring + actionable). Visual hierarchy rule: illustration > heading > description > CTA. Game-like mechanics in first-use empty states increase engagement (68% of users begin populating data immediately when shown a game-like 0% complete indicator). Empty states = highest ROI UX investment.

title: Audit Logging for Internal Tools — AppMaster
insights: Audit log pattern for enterprise tools: immutable event table (who, when, what entity, what changed, from value, to value, IP address). UI pattern: table with columns = Timestamp | User | Action | Entity | Details. Expandable row for before/after diff. Filter by user, date range, action type. Export to CSV. Retention display: 'showing last 90 days'. Anti-pattern: combining audit log with activity feed (different audiences, different needs).

title: Dashboard Design Patterns for Modern Web Apps 2025 — Art of Styleframe
insights: 2025 dashboard patterns: (1) Grid + sidebar (240px sidebar + card grid), (2) Bento box (varying-size cards, CSS grid areas), (3) Stats + table (KPI row + full-width data table), (4) Command center (monitoring dashboard, real-time updates, alert strip). Bento box pattern gaining adoption in 2024-2025 (SaaS landing pages → admin dashboards). Dark mode: default for monitoring/analytics dashboards.

title: Designing Settings Screen UI — LogRocket
insights: LogRocket settings screen patterns: single-page scroll (≤8 settings), tabbed (8-15), side-nav (15+). Control types by setting: boolean = toggle switch (not checkbox), single-select = radio group (3 options) or dropdown (4+ options), multi-select = checkbox group, range = slider. Auto-save vs explicit save: auto-save for simple toggles, explicit save for complex forms. Toast confirmation after save.

title: Modal UX Design Patterns — LogRocket
insights: LogRocket modal patterns: animation (fade + scale-up, 200ms), scroll (content scrolls inside modal, footer sticky), responsive (mobile = bottom sheet full-width, desktop = centered 80vw max). Action orientation: put primary action on right, cancel on left (platform convention: iOS = left, Android/Web = right). Close triggers: X button, Escape key, backdrop click (optional for confirmations). Form in modal: inline validation.

title: Skeleton Loading Screen Design — LogRocket
insights: Skeleton design rules: (1) Mirror final layout exactly (same widths, heights, position), (2) Vary placeholder widths (not all same width — looks unnatural), (3) Use shimmer animation (not pulse), (4) Show for initial render only (not for refresh/pagination), (5) Remove in ≤3 seconds (if longer, add progress context). Text placeholders: line-height and number of lines must match real content. Table skeleton: rows match expected page size.

title: Toast Notifications Best Practices — LogRocket
insights: Toast pattern: fixed position (top-right desktop, top or bottom-center mobile), max-width 400px, auto-dismiss (success 3-4s, info 5s, no auto-dismiss for error). Queue management: stack toasts vertically (max 3 visible), older slide up as new ones appear. Action in toast: 'Undo' for destructive actions (5s window). Icon: colored icon left of message matches severity. Content: ≤80 chars, past tense ('Saved', 'Deleted').

title: 8 Settings Page UI Examples: Design Patterns That Work — BricxLabs
insights: 8 settings pattern analysis: Stripe (side-nav + single-column form, auto-save indicators), Linear (keyboard-first navigation, dense layout, search for settings), Notion (modal-based settings separate from main content, sections: Account/Workspace/Notifications/Integrations), Vercel (tab nav + form sections, real-time preview for some settings), GitHub (left sidebar with sub-sections, security section prominent). Key differentiator: search within settings (Linear, GitHub both implement).

title: 15 Filter UI Patterns That Work in 2025 — BricxLabs
insights: 15 filter pattern implementations with code: (1) horizontal pills/chips bar, (2) dropdown select group, (3) sidebar collapsible, (4) floating filter button + drawer, (5) inline column filters (table), (6) search + filter combined, (7) date range + preset chips, (8) tag-based multi-select, (9) rating/score range slider, (10) map-based geographic filter, (11) AI natural language filter, (12) filter presets (saved views), (13) toggle switch filters, (14) color swatch filter, (15) hierarchical tree filter.

title: Interview Scheduling UX — Candidate.fyi
insights: Interview scheduling UI pattern: candidate availability request = email with calendar link showing available slots (Google Calendar / Outlook sync). Recruiter sees: combined availability view (grid of all interviewer availability merged), selects slot, confirms. Candidate self-scheduling link shows available times without showing interviewer names. ATS integration: interview event created in ATS + calendar invites sent. Reminder emails: 24h and 1h before.

title: 14 Types of Onboarding UX Used by Top Apps — DesignerUp
insights: 14 onboarding pattern types with real examples: (1) Welcome screens, (2) Account creation wizard, (3) Role/goal selection, (4) Product tour overlay, (5) Video walkthrough, (6) Interactive tutorial, (7) Empty state CTA, (8) Contextual tooltips, (9) Checklist widget, (10) Progress bar activation, (11) Sample data populated (pre-filled empty state), (12) Setup wizard, (13) Email drip onboarding, (14) Human onboarding (guided by customer success). Best performing: #11 (sample data) + #9 (checklist).

title: Spectrum 2: Adobe's Evolving Design System — Design System Central
insights: Spectrum 2 (2025) key pattern changes: adaptive color modes (light, dark, high-contrast), new density system (compact/regular/spacious), AI interaction patterns (AI chat, prompt input, streaming response display), Express-style patterns (creative tools overlay patterns). Pattern updates: action menus now support inline disclosure, dialogs support full-screen on mobile, picker supports grouping and virtual scroll for large datasets.

title: Cloudscape (Amazon) Design System Overview — DesignSystems.surf
insights: Cloudscape overview: 60+ components, 30+ pattern guidelines, 20+ full demos. Pattern strength: resource management (create, view, edit, delete resource flows fully documented), wizard (multi-step resource creation), split view (list + detail preview), inline editing. Cloudscape is the UX system behind AWS Console — optimised for complex technical interfaces. Demo apps: Create resource, Table with filtering, Wizard, Dashboard.

title: Interview Kit/Scorecard — Workable Help
insights: Workable interview kit = question set + scorecard + evaluation form combined. UI pattern: recruiter creates kit (drag-drop question ordering, competency mapping), interviewer fills during/after interview (mobile-friendly form), hiring manager sees aggregate in pipeline view. Scorecard submission required before advancing candidate in some configurations. Collaboration: comment thread on candidate record.

title: Candidate Interview Scorecards Guide — Hirevire
insights: HR-domain candidate scorecard UI pattern: competency rating matrix (N competencies × 5-point scale), overall recommendation (Strong Yes/Yes/Maybe/No/Strong No), structured comments per competency, interviewer name + timestamp. Aggregate view: multiple interviewers' scorecards averaged, color-coded. Conflict detection: flag when interviewers diverge significantly. Calibration meeting: side-by-side scorecard comparison UI. Export to PDF.

title: Data Table UX: 5 Rules of Thumb — Mann Howie
insights: 5 data table rules: (1) Right-align numbers (allows column-level comparison), (2) Monospace font for numbers (aligns decimal points), (3) Truncate long text with tooltip-on-hover (not wrapping), (4) Sticky header for 10+ visible rows, (5) Column header click = sort toggle (ASC/DESC/neutral cycle). Density variants: compact (32px rows) for power users, default (48px) for standard, comfortable (64px) for touch. Status: colored badge or colored dot + label.

title: Best UI Patterns for SaaS Dashboards — Medium
insights: SaaS dashboard pattern analysis: header strip (4 KPI cards, icon + metric + delta % + trend sparkline), chart area (line/bar/donut depending on data type), alert rail (right sidebar for notifications/tasks), data table (filtered by context). KPI card anatomy: label (gray, small) + value (large, bold) + delta (green/red + arrow + %) + mini-sparkline. Empty KPI: '--' with tooltip explaining no data.

title: Empty States for Enterprise Web Apps — Medium
insights: Enterprise empty state specifics: role-aware messaging (admin sees 'Add your first user', viewer sees 'No users have been added yet'). Permission-aware CTA (only show if user has permission to take action). Empty state within nested UI (table inside a tab inside a page): smaller illustration, 2-line max text, no large CTA button (use text link). Data export empty state: 'No exports yet — create your first report'.

title: Refactoring UI Book Summary — Medium
insights: Refactoring UI key compositions: (1) Layered shadow system (3 depths: sm=0 1px 3px, md=0 4px 6px, lg=0 10px 15px), (2) Status badge recipe = colored dot + text (vs colored pill background — pill has higher visual weight), (3) Table row hover = very subtle bg change (gray-50 light, gray-800 dark), (4) Form field recipe = label + input + helper text stack (16px between label/input, 8px input/helper). Component library has 200+ variants.

title: HR Candidate Pipeline UX Case Study — Medium
insights: HR-domain case study redesigning candidate pipeline. Key UX findings: recruiters need (1) at-a-glance stage overview (how many candidates per stage), (2) quick candidate card scan (name, position, days-in-stage, next action), (3) quick actions without modal (reject, advance, schedule), (4) bulk operations for sourcing phase. Color system: green=progressing, yellow=pending action, red=blocked/overdue, gray=rejected. Card info hierarchy: name > position > match % > days-in-stage.

title: Command Palette UX Patterns — Alicja Suska
insights: Command palette UX taxonomy: (1) Global commands (open document, switch workspace), (2) Contextual commands (actions on selected item), (3) Navigation shortcuts (go to page), (4) Settings shortcuts. Result row anatomy: icon + label + keyboard shortcut + category tag. Empty state: 'No results for X — try Y' with suggestions. Accessibility: role=listbox, each result role=option, aria-activedescendant.

title: Modern Enterprise UI Design: Modal Dialogs — Pulsar/Medium
insights: Enterprise modal dialogs deep-dive: size variants (sm/md/lg/xl), scrollable content area with fixed header/footer, form validation inside modal (inline errors + submit-triggered error summary), wizard-in-modal (for 3-5 step forms), preview modal (full-screen media viewer), iframe modal (embedding external content — anti-pattern in most cases). Mobile adaptation: modal → full-screen page for forms, modal → bottom sheet for confirmations.

title: Card UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin card analysis: card anatomy = media (top), title + metadata (middle), actions (bottom). Card click = navigate to detail. 3 content density variants: compact (title only), standard (title + description + 1 action), expanded (full metadata + 2 actions). Cards used for: content browsing (news, products), resource grids (files, projects), people directories (team, candidates). Hover state: shadow lift + border highlight.

title: Command Palette UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin command palette analysis from 200+ real apps. Activation: Cmd+K (59% of apps), Cmd+P (Visual Studio Code influence), Cmd+/ (contextual). Anatomy: search input (autofocus), grouped results (sections: Recent, Commands, Files, People), keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter). Enterprise apps: show keyboard shortcut for each command. Max results visible: 7-8 before scroll. Placeholder text = 'Search actions, files, people...'

title: Date Picker UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin date picker analysis: calendar grid (most common, 78%), wheel/scroll (iOS native, 22%), free text input (always as fallback). Date range: dual calendar (side-by-side months) on desktop, single calendar with range highlighting on mobile. Presets strip above calendar: 'Today', 'This week', 'This month', 'Custom'. Disabled dates: grayed out, not removed. Clear button in top-right corner.

title: Empty State UI Pattern — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin empty state gallery: 400+ real-world empty state screens from 1150+ apps. Pattern findings: consumer apps favor illustrated empty states with character/mascot; enterprise apps favor icon + text + CTA. Dark mode empty states use lighter illustrations. Mobile empty states: illustration fills 40-50% of screen, centered. Web empty states: centered within content area, never full-viewport. Animated empty states (Lottie) used by 23% of top apps.

title: Skeleton UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin skeleton analysis: 85% of top apps use skeleton screens vs spinners. Shimmer direction: left-to-right (63%), top-to-bottom (22%), radial (15%). Color: gray-200 base, gray-100 shimmer (light mode); gray-700 base, gray-600 shimmer (dark mode). Line placeholder widths: vary intentionally (50-90%) to look natural. Avatar placeholder: circular. Never use skeleton for sub-200ms loads (visual noise).

title: Microsoft Fluent UI Design System — Motiff
insights: Fluent UI 2 (web) patterns: DataGrid (virtualized, sortable, filterable, column resize), DatePicker, CommandBar (horizontal action bar with overflow menu), SearchBox, Panel (side drawer), Dialog, ProgressIndicator. Teams app patterns: TaskList, PeoplePicker (HR-relevant), Meeting details pattern. Fluent 2 introduces: semi-transparent effects, rounded corners, dense + regular + comfortable density modes. Microsoft 365 context means patterns are optimized for enterprise collaboration tools.

title: UI Component Examples Catalog — Nicelydone
insights: Nicelydone: 14,300+ real SaaS UI components from 214,200+ screens. Pattern categories with example counts (2025): AI components (277+), Modal (116+), Tabs (283+), Bento grid (259+), Timeline/History (155+), Autocomplete (4+), Settings pages (4167+). Weekly updated. Used by design teams at enterprise SaaS. Best source for 'what do real apps do' validation of pattern choices.

title: Timeline & History UI Examples — Nicelydone
insights: Nicelydone timeline gallery (155+ examples): vertical timeline most common. Anatomy: connector line (left-aligned vertical), event node (icon or dot), timestamp (right-aligned), event description. Grouping: by day (most common), by actor, by type. Expandable events: show truncated description, expand to full detail. Activity feed variant: real-time updates, newest first. Audit log variant: immutable, includes 'who changed what from X to Y'.

title: Command Palette Interfaces — Philip Davis
insights: Analysis of command palette evolution: originated in Sublime Text (Cmd+Shift+P), adopted by VS Code, Linear, Notion, Figma, Vercel dashboard. Pattern anatomy: modal overlay (full or centered floating), search input (autofocus, placeholder 'Search commands...'), result groups (Recent, Commands, Navigation), keyboard shortcut hints. Anti-pattern: hiding palette activation trigger — must be discoverable via UI button for new users.

title: How to Design a Paywall That Converts — Qonversion
insights: Paywall anatomy: headline (benefit-focused, not product feature), pricing table (2-3 tiers max, highlight recommended with 'Popular' badge), feature comparison checklist (tiers as columns, features as rows, checkmarks), CTA button (per tier, contrasting color), trust signals (review count, logos, refund policy). Annual/monthly toggle with savings label ('Save 40%'). Trial button separate from buy button. Social proof below CTA.

title: Kanban Board in Recruitment — Treegarden
insights: HR-domain Kanban pattern: recruitment pipeline stages = Applied, Screening, Interview 1, Interview 2, Offer, Hired/Rejected. Candidate card surface: name, role, days-in-stage (highlighted if >SLA), status badge, pending actions icon. WIP limits per stage reduce bottlenecks. Quick actions on card: move to next stage, schedule interview, reject, add note. Bulk move via drag-select + column drop. Swimlanes by hiring manager or job req.

title: File Uploader UX Best Practices — Uploadcare
insights: File upload UX: show constraints BEFORE upload (file types, max size, count) — not as error after failure. Drag zone affordance: dashed border + icon + 'Drag files here or click to browse'. Progress: individual per-file progress bars for multi-file. Preview: image thumbnail for image files, file icon + name for others. Error handling: specific messages ('File too large — max 10MB', not 'Upload failed'). Retry on failure.

title: 19 Onboarding UX Examples — Userpilot
insights: Userpilot analysis of 19 SaaS onboarding flows. Top patterns: (1) Segmentation survey on signup (4-5 questions, personalizes flow), (2) Progressive onboarding checklist (persistent widget, gamified progress), (3) Empty state onboarding (inline CTA within empty data areas), (4) Tooltip walkthrough (hotspot-triggered, not forced linear tour), (5) Success state celebration (confetti/animation on first value moment). HR-specific: ATS onboarding shows candidate pipeline setup.

title: Date Picker Design — UX Collective
insights: UX Collective date picker analysis: grid calendar beats wheel for date range selection (easier to grasp start/end). Mandatory fields: always provide free-text input alongside calendar. Keyboard: arrow keys navigate calendar cells, Enter selects, Page Up/Down changes month. Date range: first click = start, second click = end, highlighted in between. Time picker: separate from date picker (avoid combined complex component).

title: Best Practices for Actions in Data Tables — UX Design World
insights: Data table action patterns: row actions placement options — inline (always visible), revealed-on-hover (less visual noise), in a contextual menu (overflow). Primary action in first/last column as button. Secondary actions in overflow menu (3-dot icon). Bulk actions: toolbar buttons, not in row menus. Dangerous row actions: confirmable inline (undo snackbar) or modal. Anti-pattern: too many inline buttons per row (>3 creates visual noise).

title: Designing a Perfect Date Picker — UX Design World
insights: Date picker guidelines: calendar grid vs text input — always provide both. Calendar: prev/next month arrows, year/month dropdown for quick navigation, today highlighted, selected date highlighted, disabled dates grayed. Range: hover highlights interim dates. Footer: Today button (quick-set) + Clear button. Accessibility: arrow keys navigate cells, Enter selects, Escape closes, focus returns to trigger. ARIA: role=grid, aria-selected on selected date.

title: Inline Editing in Tables — UX Design World
insights: Inline edit in tables: 3 modes — cell-click (click cell to edit), icon-trigger (pencil icon in last column), row-click (click row to enter edit mode for all fields). Save patterns: (1) explicit save button (safe, no data loss), (2) blur/focus-out auto-save (fast, risky), (3) Enter key save + Escape cancel (keyboard-first). Validation in edit mode: red border + inline message below cell. Cell type adaptations: text=input, boolean=checkbox, select=dropdown.

title: Timeline Pattern — UX Patterns for Developers
insights: UX Patterns dev timeline pattern: three types: vertical summary (compact list, date + icon + description), horizontal (milestones along time axis), swimlane (multiple actors, Gantt-like). Implementation considerations: lazy-load for long timelines, grouping reduces cognitive load, filter/search on large timelines, 'show more' expansion pattern. Real-time feed: WebSocket update inserts new item at top with subtle animation.

title: Best UX Practices for Designing a Sidebar — UX Planet
insights: Sidebar UX: icon + text is always better than icon-only (comprehension 35% faster). Tooltips required for icon-only collapsed state. Max 7 primary nav items (Miller's Law). Group items with section headers for 8+ items. Active item: accent color fill, not just underline (strong affordance). Nested nav: accordion sub-items, indent 16px. Footer area: user profile + settings gear + logout. Horizontal line separator between sections.

title: Sidebar Design for Web Apps — ALF Design Group
insights: Sidebar pattern composition: SidebarProvider (context) + SidebarNav (items with icon + label + badge) + SidebarFooter (user avatar + logout) + CollapseToggle (persist to localStorage). State: expanded (240px, icon+label), icon-only (64px, tooltip on hover), hidden (0, toggle visible). Nav item states: default, hover (subtle bg), active (accent bg, bold text), disabled. Keyboard nav: Tab through items, Enter to navigate. Responsive: ≤768px = drawer overlay.

title: Freemium Upgrade Prompts — Appcues
insights: Upgrade prompt patterns from 50 top SaaS apps: in-context lock icon on premium feature (lowest friction, highest conversion), upgrade modal triggered at limit hit, feature discovery prompt ('You're about to use a Pro feature'), usage bar with upgrade CTA at 80% limit. Message framing research: 'Unlock [benefit]' outperforms 'Upgrade to Pro' by 31%. Always show trial option alongside paid. Dismiss must be possible.

title: User Onboarding UI/UX Patterns — Appcues
insights: Appcues onboarding pattern taxonomy: Modals (welcome, feature announcement), Tooltips (contextual hotspot, progressive), Slideouts (side panel tutorial), Checklists (sticky widget with tasks), Driven actions (highlight + restrict navigation), Empty states with CTAs (setup prompts in context). Key principle: get users to 'aha moment' within first session. Personalization via signup survey reduces time-to-value by 48%.

title: Designing Chronological Activity Feeds — Aubergine Solutions
insights: Activity feed design guide: anatomy = avatar/icon + actor name + action verb + object + timestamp. Grouping: by day separator. Aggregation: 'User A and 3 others commented' collapses multiple same-type events. Real-time: new items insert at top with subtle slide-down animation. Load more: 'Show 20 older activities' button (not infinite scroll for audit trails). Filtering by actor, type, date range. Export to CSV.

title: Onboarding UX Patterns — Chameleon
insights: Chameleon onboarding pattern detail: Checklist widget = persistent element (bottom-left corner), shows tasks with completion state. Tooltip hotspot = pulsing dot on UI element, click to reveal guidance. Modal flow = full-screen overlay, 3-5 steps, progress dots. Slideout = 300px side panel, triggered by action. Tooltip vs modal: tooltips for contextual help, modals for new feature announcements. Conditional branching based on user role.

title: Bulk Action UX: 8 Design Guidelines for SaaS — Eleken
insights: 8 SaaS bulk action guidelines: (1) Floating bottom toolbar pattern beats static toolbar for large tables. (2) Show '{N} selected' counter. (3) Allow select all across pages. (4) Group actions by type (primary, secondary, destructive). (5) Disable unavailable bulk actions contextually. (6) Undo toast after destructive bulk action. (7) Progress indicator for long-running bulk ops. (8) Empty selection: toolbar disappears.

title: Empty State UX Examples and Design Rules — Eleken
insights: Eleken analysis of 30+ real SaaS empty states. Key finding: best empty states use action-oriented headlines ('Start by adding your first candidate') not declarative ones ('No candidates yet'). Illustration style: abstract/geometric beats photorealistic for empty states. First-time empty states: celebratory tone. Filter/search no-results: problem-solving tone ('Try removing some filters'). Never show empty state for load errors — use error state instead.

title: Modal UX Design: Patterns and Best Practices — Eleken
insights: Eleken modal analysis: when to use modal vs drawer vs page: modal = quick task (3-5 fields), requires immediate attention; drawer = contextual detail without leaving page (Salesforce pattern); page = complex form (10+ fields). Modal sizes: small (400px, confirmations), medium (600px, forms), large (800px, wizards/previews). Header: title + X button. Footer: primary + secondary buttons. Never put modals inside modals.

title: User Onboarding UX Patterns — Eleken
insights: SaaS onboarding patterns: product tour (step-by-step UI walkthrough via tooltips), interactive checklist (persistent sidebar widget, tasks with checkmarks + completion %), empty state onboarding (first-use empty state with inline setup prompt), modal-based welcome flow (3-5 step full-screen wizard on first login). Best performers: checklist (72% completion rate) > product tour (38%). Always allow skip.

title: Wizard UI Pattern — Eleken
insights: Wizard pattern analysis with 20+ real examples. When to use: multi-variable setup (3+ interdependent fields), legal/compliance forms, onboarding (personalization required). When NOT to use: simple 1-2 field forms, repeated daily tasks, when users need to review all fields together. Personalisation wizards: branch on user answers to skip irrelevant steps (conditional logic).

title: Figma Community Libraries — Free Template Libraries
insights: Figma Community: 4,700+ UI kits, 1,000+ template libraries. Top pattern-focused libraries: Untitled UI (320k+ users, 10k components), TapTap DS (dark mode, Auto Layout 5.0), shadcn/ui kit (code export plugin). Pattern recommendation by use: SaaS = Untitled UI; Enterprise = Ant Design or Carbon kit; Government = USWDS kit. Figma now supports component properties, variables for theming, and advanced prototyping for pattern documentation.

title: Multi-Step Form UX Best Practices — Growform
insights: Multi-step form research: completion rates increase 87% when form is split into logical steps vs one long page. Progress bar placement: top of form (not bottom). Step naming: descriptive labels ('Contact info', 'Job details') not numbered only ('Step 1/5'). Back navigation: always allow, preserve entered values. Optional fields: mark as '(optional)', never skip steps that have required fields without validation. Final review step increases accuracy.

title: Dashboard Design Best Practices — Justinmind
insights: Dashboard UX patterns: KPI strip (top row, 4-6 metric cards), charts row (trend over time), data table (detail below charts). Information hierarchy: summary → trend → detail. Progressive disclosure: KPI card click → drilldown view. Date range filter affects all widgets. Responsive grid: 4-col desktop → 2-col tablet → 1-col mobile. Widget customization: show/hide panels, drag to reorder. Empty widget state: 'No data for selected period'.

title: Empty States UI Design Best Practices — SetProduct
insights: Setproduct empty state content guidelines: illustration library pairing (each common empty state has a recommended illustration). Message formula: headline (active voice, 20 chars max) + body (2-3 lines, benefit-focused) + CTA (verb+noun). First-use tone: warm and encouraging. No-results tone: helpful and diagnostic. Anti-pattern: 'You have no items' — passive and unhelpful. Error empty state: avoid blame language.

title: Notifications UI Design Best Practices — SetProduct
insights: SetProduct notification taxonomy: ephemeral (auto-dismiss: success, info), persistent (require dismiss: warning, error), passive (no dismiss, background info). Toast position best practices: top-right for desktop (away from primary content), bottom-center for mobile. Notification center (bell icon): unread count badge, drawer with all notifications, mark-all-read action. Push notification permission flow: context + timing best practices.

title: App Settings UI Design — SetProduct
insights: Settings design patterns: layout decision tree — under 8 sections: single scroll page; 8-15 sections: tabbed or left-nav; 15+ sections: nested left-nav with search. Settings search (instant filter of settings labels) dramatically improves findability in complex apps (e.g., system preferences). Always show current value for each setting. Destructive settings: visually separated, red border section, explicit warning.

title: SaaS Onboarding Flow — Userflow
insights: Complete SaaS onboarding flow anatomy: (1) Sign-up page (minimal friction, social auth), (2) Welcome survey (2-4 questions, role/goal/company size), (3) Account setup (profile, team invite, integration connect), (4) Value delivery (guided first action), (5) Checklist (persistent reminder of unfinished tasks). Time to value metric: goal is <5 minutes to first 'aha moment'. Emails: confirmation, welcome, 3-day check-in sequence.

title: History of Command Palettes — Vendr
insights: Command palette evolution timeline: TextMate (2005, first implementation), Sublime Text (2011, Cmd+Shift+P popularized), Atom/VS Code (2014-2015, developer tooling standard), GitHub (2021, global /command-k for navigation), Linear (2021, product management standard), Raycast (2021, OS-level command palette), Figma (2022, resource lookup + actions). Pattern has migrated from dev tools → productivity SaaS → enterprise apps. Cmd+K now the de facto shortcut.

title: Paywall UX Design Best Practices — Webuild
insights: Paywall pattern anatomy: feature preview (show what's locked), value proposition (3-5 bullet points of premium benefits), pricing table (2-3 tiers max, highlight recommended), CTA button (trial or subscribe), trust signals (logos, testimonials, money-back). Soft paywall converts 3.2x better than hard. Contextual paywall (triggered at point of blocked action) converts 2.1x better than dedicated upgrade page.

title: Atlassian Design System Overview — Design Systems Surf
insights: Atlassian DS overview: 50+ components, pattern library for Jira/Confluence/Trello. 2025 typography refresh (bolder fonts). Key patterns: issue creation flow (quick-create modal vs full-page), page tree navigation (Confluence), board view (Jira Kanban), backlog prioritization. AI patterns: Rovo chat interface, AI suggestion inline in editor. Atlassian uses progressive enhancement: basic functionality without JS, enhanced with JS.

title: React: Building Multi-Step Form with Wizard Pattern — Medium
insights: React wizard implementation patterns: Context API for shared step state, useReducer for step navigation, per-step validation with Yup/Zod, step registration via hook (dynamic wizards). URL-based step tracking (?step=2) for deep linking and refresh safety. Animation: step transition via framer-motion (slide-left/right). Schema: each step validates only its own fields. Final review step: renders all previous step summaries as read-only.

title: Designing Profile, Account, and Settings Pages — Medium Bootcamp
insights: Settings page structure: Profile (avatar, display name, bio, pronouns), Account (email, password, 2FA, connected accounts), Notifications (channel preferences: email/push/SMS per notification type), Privacy (data export, data deletion, cookie preferences), Billing (plan, payment method, invoices), Integrations (connected apps with revoke), Danger zone (delete account, deactivate). Dangerous zone: visually separated, red border or different background, require typing account name to confirm delete.

title: Accordion UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin accordion patterns: chevron indicator (right-aligned, 78%); plus/minus indicator (15%); arrow (7%). Expand/collapse animation: max-height transition (smooth) vs display none toggle (instant). Mobile accordions: full-width, chevron right. Desktop accordions: may be partial-width. Single vs multi-expand: settings pages usually single-open, FAQ pages usually multi-open. Divider between items: 1px border.

title: SaaS Onboarding Design Checklist — Procreator Design
insights: Comprehensive SaaS onboarding checklist: sign-up friction (OAuth options, minimal fields), first login (welcome modal or empty state with CTA), profile setup (optional, delay until value experienced), feature discovery (contextual vs linear), activation milestone (first meaningful action), email nurture (3-part sequence: welcome, tip, re-engage), retention hooks (saved searches, notification preferences, team invite). Checklist pattern: show after first login, dismiss when all tasks complete.

title: How to Design Inline Edit Feature — WebAppHuddle
insights: Inline edit affordance patterns: (1) dashed border on hover (Notion-style), (2) pencil icon on hover (Microsoft 365 style), (3) underline text (clickable text affordance), (4) edit badge on view-mode field. Mixed-mode form: some fields always-edit, some display-until-clicked. Inline edit for tables: only practical for ≤5 editable columns; more than that should use row edit or detail drawer.

title: 150+ Free Stepper/Wizard Component Types — Figma Community
insights: Figma resource: 150+ stepper/wizard component variants. Categories: horizontal stepper (numbered, labeled, icon), vertical stepper (left-nav pattern), breadcrumb stepper, progress bar variant, dot indicator. States: inactive, active, completed, error. Mobile variants included. Demonstrates the breadth of wizard pattern implementations across industries. Used as reference for custom pattern implementations.
